summary: Decompiled and typed several character item routines for Ice Climbers Blizzard plus Master Hand Bullet and Master Hand Laser. The PR replaced placeholder/UNK declarations with concrete C implementations, item variable structs, attribute structs, item-var union entries, and more precise headers. It also made a small Dr. Mario Pill cleanup by replacing manual 2D velocity squared math with VEC2_SQ_LEN. There was no substantive review text in the supplied slice, so lessons are inferred from the diff rather than reviewer commentary.
what_changed: Added itClimbersBlizzard_ItemVars and itClimbersBlizzardAttributes to itCharItems.h, then added climbersblizzard to Item.xDD4_itemVar in it/types.h.

Implemented Ice Climbers Blizzard item routines in itclimbersblizzard.c, including spawn path it_802C2144, velocity/angle setup in it_802C2248, animation update wrapper chain, lifetime decrement logic, physics/collision callbacks, and parent/ref cleanup via it_8026B894.
Added a local GET_ATTRS macro for Blizzard that casts ip->xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes to itClimbersBlizzardAttributes.

Implemented Master Hand Bullet routines in itmasterhandbullet.c, including spawn setup it_802F0AE0, state initialization helpers it_802F0BE8 and it_802F0D2C, timer/effect update it_802F0E70, collision/physics callbacks, and accessory no-op callbacks.
Added itMasterHandBullet_ItemVars and itMasterHandBulletAttributes and wired masterhandbullet into Item.xDD4_itemVar.

Implemented Master Hand Laser routines in itmasterhandlaser.c, including spawn/attachment to a Fighter_Part in it_802F0340, state init it_802F0484, end condition it_802F04F0, accessory update it_802F05A8, and joint/collision-length adjustment in it_802F063C.
Expanded itMasterHandLaser_ItemVars from a single bool to fields for active/end flag, fighter part, effect timer, and attached HSD_JObj pointer, and added itMasterHandLaserAttributes.
Updated Master Hand Bullet and Laser headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or loose u32 parameters to concrete Item_GObj, Vec3, Fighter_Part, ItemKind, f32, bool, and void signatures.
In itdrmariopill.c, included baselib/mtx.h and replaced manual x/y velocity squared expression with VEC2_SQ_LEN(ip->x40_vel).
smart_moves: Modeled per-item xDD4 data with named item-var structs and union members instead of leaving raw offsets, making future item routines easier to type and audit.
Pulled specialAttributes into explicit attribute structs, preserving offset-based field names where semantics were not yet known rather than overnaming unknown data.

Used spawn helper patterns matching the item subsystem: fill SpawnItem, set parent fields, mark x44_flag.b0, call Item_80268B18, then run debug/linking and item setup calls such as db_80225DD8 and it_802750F8.

Preserved matching-oriented inline/control-flow structure, including the Blizzard no-inline wrapper chain around it_802C2358 and a static inline it_802F0AE0_sub for Master Hand Bullet state selection.

Attached Master Hand Laser to the owning fighter part by storing Fighter_Part in item vars and using lb_8000C2F8 against fp->parts[part].joint, giving the accessory update enough typed state to recompute positions.
Used existing math/vector macro VEC2_SQ_LEN in Dr. Mario Pill instead of duplicating x*x + y*y, reducing repeated expression noise.
decomp_lessons: When decompiling item articles, first establish the xDD4 item variable layout and add the corresponding union member in Item; this unlocks clearer field access in callbacks.
Article special attributes should be represented as small typed structs even if fields remain x0/x4/x8; the cast from xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes centralizes attribute interpretation.
Spawn functions in the item system often require exact initialization order and fields: kind, prev_pos, pos, facing_dir, damage, velocity, parent gobj, duplicate parent pointer, x44 flag, and x40.
For matching, wrapper functions and apparently redundant no-op callbacks may be necessary because item state tables or function pointer fields reference exact symbols.

Master Hand Laser demonstrates a common attached-effect pattern: store the owner part/joint, copy or bind transforms, update dynamic bones every accessory callback, and clamp/adjust the visual endpoint based on collision checks.
Use established project macros such as GET_ITEM, GET_JOBJ, GET_FIGHTER, ITEM_ANIM_UPDATE, and VEC2_SQ_LEN to align with surrounding code style and reduce matching risk from handwritten equivalents.

The slice shows one likely signature inconsistency to watch: itmasterhandbullet.c defines it_802F0ED8 as returning bool, while the shown header change declares it as void. Future work should verify final merged headers or correct prototypes before reuse.
naming_conventions: Per-item variable structs use the pattern it<Name>_ItemVars, for example itClimbersBlizzard_ItemVars and itMasterHandBullet_ItemVars.
Special attribute structs use the pattern it<Name>Attributes, for example itClimbersBlizzardAttributes, itMasterHandBulletAttributes, and itMasterHandLaserAttributes.
Unknown fields remain offset-style names such as x0, x4, x8, xC, and x10 when no semantic name is proven.
Item union members use lowercase item names such as climbersblizzard, masterhandbullet, and masterhandlaser.
Local attribute access can use a GET_ATTRS(ip) macro when multiple routines in a file repeatedly cast specialAttributes.
Function prototypes retain address-derived names such as it_802C2144, it_802F0AE0, and it_802F063C until semantic names are established.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced M_TAU as 6.283185307179586 and used M_PI_2 plus while-based angle wrapping for Blizzard velocity setup.
Used HSD_Randf, cosf, and sinf to derive Blizzard initial velocity from randomized attribute-controlled angles and facing direction.
Added a chain of no-inline wrapper functions around it_802C2358, likely to preserve call structure or symbol boundaries needed for matching.
Used Quaternion initialization with quad.y = 1.5707963267948966 * ip->facing_dir before HSD_JObjSetRotation in Master Hand Bullet setup.
Kept empty callback functions such as it_802F0F04, it_802F0F08, it_802F0ED4, and it_802F059C because item state/accessory tables may require distinct function symbols.
Used PAD_STACK markers and a local my_sqrtf implementation in Master Hand Laser, both common decomp tactics for stack shape and floating-point matching.
Recomputed Master Hand Laser endpoint translation from joint world positions, mpLib_80051EC8 collision query results, and sqrtf distance ratios, matching the original visual clipping behavior.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the supplied PR slice.
The only human activity count indicated one issue comment, but no body text excerpt was available, so no reviewer intent or requested changes can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Check final merged itmasterhandbullet.h and itmasterhandbullet.c for the it_802F0ED8 return type mismatch suggested by the diff excerpt.
Search for later PRs that rename x0/x4/x8 fields in itClimbersBlizzardAttributes, itMasterHandBulletAttributes, or itMasterHandLaserAttributes.
Compare the Blizzard no-inline wrapper chain against adjacent item PRs to determine whether this became a reusable matching tactic.
Look for subsequent decomp of Master Hand item state tables referencing it_802F0F04, it_802F0F08, it_802F05A8, or it_802F063C.
